# interlink_ip_transit (Resource)

Manages an Inter.link IP Transit service. Note: this service cannot be cancelled through the API — `terraform destroy` will fail by design (see the delete behaviour). Use `terraform state rm` to stop managing it without cancelling.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Manage an IP Transit service delivered on a newly provisioned port.
resource "interlink_ip_transit" "example" {
  bgpsession_asn             = 64500 # your public AS number
  bgpsession_as_set          = "AS-EXAMPLE"
  bgpsession_password        = var.bgp_password # sensitive; write-only
  bgpsession_prefix_limit_v4 = 1000
  bgpsession_prefix_limit_v6 = 100
  prefix_v4_size             = 30  # 30 or 31
  prefix_v6_size             = 126 # 126 or 127
  term                       = 12  # months
  sync_from_pdb              = true

  # Provide exactly one port block: new_port, existing_port, or existing_lag.
  new_port {
    location  = "FRA1-DE"
    bandwidth = 1000 # committed data rate in Mbps
    port_type = "10G-LR (SFP+)"
    vlan_id   = 100
    vlan_type = "Tagged"
  }
}

# Attach to an existing port instead of provisioning a new one:
#
#   existing_port {
#     id        = 42
#     bandwidth = 1000
#     vlan_id   = 100
#     vlan_type = "Tagged"
#   }
#
# ...or to an existing LAG:
#
#   existing_lag {
#     lag_id    = 7
#     bandwidth = 1000
#   }
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `bgpsession_as_set` (String) AS-SET name according to RFC2622.
- `bgpsession_asn` (Number) Customer BGP autonomous system number, in the range 1-4294967295.
- `bgpsession_password` (String, Sensitive) BGP session password (MD5). Required by the API. Write-only — never read back from the API.
- `bgpsession_prefix_limit_v4` (Number) IPv4 prefix limit, in the range 0-140000.
- `bgpsession_prefix_limit_v6` (Number) IPv6 prefix limit, in the range 0-70000.
- `prefix_v4_size` (Number) Requested IPv4 prefix size (CIDR length): `30` or `31`.
- `prefix_v6_size` (Number) Requested IPv6 prefix size (CIDR length): `126` or `127`.
- `sync_from_pdb` (Boolean) Whether to sync the BGP configuration from PeeringDB.
- `term` (Number) Contract term in months.

### Optional

- `aggregated_billing` (Boolean) Whether to bill this service as part of an aggregated commit.
- `existing_lag` (Block List) Attach this service to an existing LAG. Exactly one of `new_port`, `existing_port`, or `existing_lag` must be set. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--existing_lag))
- `existing_port` (Block List) Attach this service to an existing port. Exactly one of `new_port`, `existing_port`, or `existing_lag` must be set. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--existing_port))
- `new_port` (Block List) Provision a new port for this service. Exactly one of `new_port`, `existing_port`, or `existing_lag` must be set. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--new_port))
- `outbound_advertisement` (String) Outbound BGP advertisement policy: `Default Route`, `Full Routing Table`, `Full Routing Table and Default Route`, `None - Outbound Only`, or `not set`.
- `purchase_reference` (String) Free-text purchase reference recorded against the order.

### Read-Only

- `customer_gid` (String) Customer global ID that owns the service.
- `end_date` (String) Contract end date. Answers when the service can be cancelled without early-termination charges.
- `id` (Number) Numeric service ID. Primary identifier used for read, update, and import.
- `name` (String) Service name.
- `notice_period` (Number) Cancellation notice period in days.
- `renewal_period` (Number) Automatic renewal period in months.
- `service_speed` (Number) Effective committed data rate (CDR) in Mbps.
- `sid` (String) Human-readable service ID (e.g. `SID1194`).
- `status` (String) Service status. A new order starts at `DraftQuote` and advances to `Live` out of band; it catches up on later refreshes.

<a id="nestedblock--existing_lag"></a>
### Nested Schema for `existing_lag`

Required:

- `bandwidth` (Number) Committed data rate (CDR) in Mbps, in the range 0-400000.
- `lag_id` (Number) Numeric ID of the existing LAG to attach to.

Optional:

- `vlan_id` (Number) VLAN ID in the range 1-4094. Configured here but returned at the top level on read.
- `vlan_type` (String) VLAN tagging mode: `Tagged` or `Untagged`.


<a id="nestedblock--existing_port"></a>
### Nested Schema for `existing_port`

Required:

- `bandwidth` (Number) Committed data rate (CDR) in Mbps, in the range 0-400000.
- `id` (Number) Numeric ID of the existing port to attach to.

Optional:

- `vlan_id` (Number) VLAN ID in the range 1-4094. Configured here but returned at the top level on read.
- `vlan_type` (String) VLAN tagging mode: `Tagged` or `Untagged`.


<a id="nestedblock--new_port"></a>
### Nested Schema for `new_port`

Required:

- `bandwidth` (Number) Committed data rate (CDR) in Mbps, in the range 0-400000. This is the service speed.
- `location` (String) Location name where the new port is provisioned.
- `port_type` (String) Physical port type, e.g. `100G-LR4 (QSFP28)` or `10G-LR (SFP+)`.

Optional:

- `lag_member_count` (Number) Number of member ports when the new port is a LAG.
- `lag_name` (String) Name for the LAG when the new port is a LAG.
- `vlan_id` (Number) VLAN ID in the range 1-4094. Configured here but returned at the top level on read.
- `vlan_type` (String) VLAN tagging mode: `Tagged` or `Untagged`.
